name: Find and fix a race condition in concurrent counter
task: The file /tmp/eval_race/counter.py contains a Counter class with a race condition: when multiple threads call increment() concurrently, the final value is less than expected because the read-modify-write isn't atomic. Fix the Counter class so that 10 threads each calling increment() 10000 times produces a final value of EXACTLY 100000. Do not change the public API (value attribute, increment method, run function). You may add internal synchronization.

validation.script: import importlib.util, threading; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_race/counter.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); c = mod.Counter(); threads = [threading.Thread(target=mod.run, args=(c, 10000)) for _ in range(10)]; [t.start() for t in threads]; [t.join() for t in threads]; assert c.value == 100000, f'race not fixed: got {c.value}, expected 100000'; print('OK')
